Title: Jasmonate activates a CsMPK6-CsMYC2 module that regulates the expression of β-citraurin biosynthetic genes and fruit coloration in orange (Citrus sinensis)

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates β-citraurin accumulation and peel coloration by attenuating CsMYC2 activity; overexpression reduces β-citraurin and target gene expression.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: MeJA-induced in fruit peel; expression peaks around 5 d after treatment and then declines.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: fruit peel during MeJA treatment; similar temporal pattern to CsMYC2
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: methyl jasmonate (MeJA) treatment; JA signaling context
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; pull-down; CoIP; luciferase complementation; ChIP-PCR for upstream regulation by CsMYC2; in vitro and in vivo phosphorylation assays; cell-free degradation assay; transient overexpression in citrus fruit; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsMPK6 is transcriptionally activated by CsMYC2 after MeJA treatment. 2. CsMPK6 physically interacts with CsMYC2 and phosphorylates it. 3. CsMPK6 decreases CsMYC2 stability and DNA-binding/transactivation activity, thereby reducing β-citraurin biosynthesis and fruit coloration as a negative feedback regulator.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for reducing negative feedback to enhance peel coloration and β-citraurin accumulation; caution that dampening MPK6-mediated control may overactivate JA responses.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May prevent overreaction to jasmonate; reducing its activity could disturb JA homeostasis or stress-response balance.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common_Gene because it is a signaling kinase rather than a pathway enzyme or transcription factor. MeJA enhances CsMPK6-CsMYC2 interaction.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: β-cryptoxanthin and/or related hydroxylated β,β-xanthophyll precursor(s) in β-citraurin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative cleavage in the final step leading to β-citraurin formation from carotenoid precursor(s).
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes β-citraurin accumulation and red peel coloration.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression parallels β-citraurin accumulation during fruit development and is induced by MeJA, peaking at 5 DAT.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: fruit peel during maturation and MeJA treatment
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native promoter analyzed; LUC reporter driven by CsCCD4b promoter; overexpression/silencing manipulations were on upstream regulators rather than CsCCD4b itself
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; ChIP-PCR; Y1H; EMSA; biolayer interferometry; dual-LUC; carotenoid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA treatment; JA biosynthesis inhibition by DIECA indirectly lowers pathway activation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering peel coloration and β-citraurin enrichment; promoter E-box status and expression strength are critical design points.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsCCD4b is the key biosynthetic gene for β-citraurin formation. 2. MeJA induces its expression in fruit peel. 3. CsMYC2 directly binds and activates the CsCCD4b promoter, while CsMPK6 attenuates this activation through CsMYC2.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previous work cited in the paper showed a MITE-derived E-box in the promoter enhances expression and β-citraurin accumulation in β-citraurin-enriched citrus varieties.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses geranylgeranyl diphosphate molecules to form phytoene, supplying the carotenoid precursor pool.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not established in this paper for β-citraurin specifically
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulation contributes to increased upstream carotenoid flux and promotes β-citraurin biosynthesis under MeJA/CsMYC2 activation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by MeJA with highest transcript level at 5 DAT.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; ChIP-PCR; EMSA; dual-LUC; carotenoid profiling in regulator perturbation assays
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful in pathway-stacking approaches with downstream genes such as BCH and CCD4b to boost carotenoid flux toward β-citraurin.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsPSY is induced by MeJA in orange peel. 2. CsMYC2 directly binds and activates its promoter. 3. Its upregulation supports precursor formation for β-citraurin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Treated as core pathway gene because its promoter was directly tested and its expression changed consistently with β-citraurin accumulation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes lycopene to form β-carotene in the β-branch of the carotenoid pathway.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms precursor pool for target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes β-carotene formation and thereby supports β-citraurin biosynthesis and peel coloration.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by MeJA, peaking at 5 DAT.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; ChIP-PCR; EMSA; dual-LUC; carotenoid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable engineering target to channel flux into β-carotene-derived orange/red apocarotenoids when combined with BCH and CCD4b.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsLCYb transcript abundance is increased by MeJA. 2. CsMYC2 directly binds and activates the CsLCYb promoter. 3. Increased CsLCYb expression supports accumulation of β-carotene-derived intermediates for β-citraurin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: β-carotene strongly accumulated after MeJA treatment, consistent with increased LCYb function.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: β-xanthophyll branch leading to β-citraurin precursor formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates β-carotene to produce β-cryptoxanthin/related xanthophyll intermediates used for β-citraurin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of hydroxylated carotenoid precursors required for β-citraurin production.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by MeJA with peak expression at 5 DAT.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; ChIP-PCR; EMSA; dual-LUC; carotenoid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful with LCYb and CCD4b in multi-gene engineering to increase β-citraurin content and peel coloration.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsBCH is induced by MeJA in orange peel. 2. CsMYC2 directly binds and activates the CsBCH promoter. 3. Elevated CsBCH expression supports production of β-citraurin precursor carotenoids such as β-cryptoxanthin.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: β-cryptoxanthin and violaxanthin increased after MeJA treatment, consistent with activation of BCH-linked flux.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: carotenoid biosynthesis/β-citraurin biosynthesis and feedback attenuation of JA-responsive pathway activation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates CsCCD4b, CsPSY, CsLCYb, CsBCH, and CsMPK6 transcription by promoter binding; MeJA enhances this activation.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: jasmonate/MeJA; reduced by JA biosynthesis inhibitor DIECA
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Activates CsMPK6, which feeds back negatively by binding and phosphorylating CsMYC2, decreasing its stability and promoter-binding activity.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master JA-response activator and indispensable positive regulator for JA-induced β-citraurin accumulation and peel coloration.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes β-citraurin accumulation and red coloration by directly activating multiple biosynthetic genes; silencing reduces coloration, overexpression enhances it.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in red peel at 240 DAFB; induced by MeJA and reduced by DIECA; peaks around 5 DAT.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: fruit peel during maturation and MeJA treatment
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA treatment; DIECA inhibition of JA biosynthesis; CHX/MG132 used in protein stability assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; transient overexpression and silencing in citrus fruit; subcellular localization; Y1H; EMSA; biolayer interferometry; ChIP-PCR; dual-LUC; pull-down/CoIP/LUC complementation; phosphorylation assays; degradation assays; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter; pRI101 overexpression construct (constitutive); native promoters of target genes tested in LUC/ChIP
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Prime target to enhance citrus peel coloration and β-citraurin accumulation; could be combined with structural genes or edited for reduced MPK6-mediated repression.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overactivation of JA responses may be harmful; negative feedback likely protects fruit from overreacting to jasmonate.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsMYC2 is the major JA-responsive MYC expressed in colored orange peel. 2. It directly binds and activates promoters of CsCCD4b, CsPSY, CsLCYb, and CsBCH, increasing β-citraurin pathway output. 3. It also activates CsMPK6, establishing a negative feedback loop that limits its own activity and stability.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Binding to the CsCCD4b promoter involves an E-box created by a MITE-associated SNP reported previously; MeJA enhances promoter binding in vivo.
